hints.text: Mostly **desert**; middle & south flat, north coast **dry mountains** (UAE-like), south coast greener mountains; drivers covered many **small desert tracks**

hints.uniq_note: Within the covered Middle East, yellow private plates = Oman (Israel's yellow plates carry a blue strip; Laos' yellow plates are in SE Asia).

hints.text: **White pickup truck**, mostly fully blurred (sometimes antenna/front visible)
**Arabic + English** on signs (like UAE); blue directional signs w/ white text
**Yellow outer + white middle lines**; **black-yellow kerbs** common

shared_with.diff: UAE flatter/more urban; pole and bin details decide.

hints.text: Light stone **blocky but ornate houses** w/ matching fence + ornate gate (UAE similar)
**Black-white striped signposts** (Middle East: only Oman & UAE); wooden poles w/ 3 designs — the **metal triangle above the pole** is unique to Oman; poles generally commoner than UAE/Qatar
hints.uniq_note: The triangle-topped wooden pole is unique to Oman; the plain 3-insulator design is shared with the UAE.

hints.text: Chevrons **black-yellow with unusually many arrows**; red-white painted **pylons** common (rarely Qatar)
Garbage bins black plastic or grey metal w/ 3-leaf logo + **'be'ah'** text (UAE variant: 'bee'ah', more colourful); taxis white w/ **orange side panels**

**Regions**: north = dry jagged mountains (N of Nizwa unusually shrubby); around **Salalah** = rolling hills, rocky ground, green valleys, coastal plain; NE (S/W of Bidiyah) = sandy desert w/ light shrubs + dirt roads through orange-pink dunes (SSW direction); red dunes → Saudi border + inland
Dry, slightly hilly, grey soil + brown shrubs; Road 51 has only a **white centre line**; front camera slightly **smudged**

**Vegetation**: acacias (umbrella crowns) → north + coasts; date palms → north coast; coconut palms → Salalah; gas pipelines along roads → around Fahud + central-south pockets; oil drills → Amal, Road 39, NW of Fahud

**Bin metas**: colour map by region; bin **numbers = governorate + subdivision codes**; sticker combos: black+black sticker → Al Buraimi/Ash Sharqiyah N/Muscat; grey+black → Ad Dhahirah; black+white → Ad Dakhiliyah/S-Dhofar/Muscat; grey+white → Ad Dakhiliyah (+Dhofar); black no sticker → Al Batinah S + N of Al Khaburah; grey no sticker → Al Batinah N; small western-style black → Muscat + E-Al-Batinah-N; chevron stickers on the side → Muscat; ANY side sticker → north-coast governorates
**Antenna metas**: upward antenna → around/west of Muscat + NW highways (+Al Buraimi, Sohar); forward-facing antenna → only Muscat area (copyright-year splits exist but update on refresh)

**Spotlights**: Mirbat ridge (more rugged than Salalah's); crown-shaped lone mountain SE of Nizwa; Adam = hazy, ridge+two hills north (north of Adam: mountains all around); Ubar = green farm patches in desert; Al Amarat (SE of Muscat) = urban ringed by mountains; **Fahud** = bumpy ridge + oil infrastructure; **Musandam exclave** = UAE-like extra-dry orange mountains w/ dipping beds; coastal Musandam = rocky cliffs, coast mostly west

bullets: Ibri→Saudi: flat pink-grey desert, wide shoulders, great condition, straight NE (north section curvier)
W of Fahud: same soil but poor road, no lines, SE direction
Road 29 S of Ibri: shrubby, low jagged mountains east, low sun NW; southern part = only white middle line
Road 31: light flat desert, wide shoulders, faded lines, cloudy; its divided section = the only divided desert road
Road 32 N of Duqm: hilly desert, northbound; into Ash Sharqiyah N: wide shoulders + darker shrubbier desert, partly overcast
Road 35: hills+green shrubs, curved lamp posts NE side
Road 37: NW angle, power line NE (south half), sunset (north half)
Road 41 coastal: lightest sand, coast visible, ridge west; western stretch = wide shoulders + reddish soil, cloudy east
Road 42/49 E of Salalah: scenic coast, tall light rocky cliffs
S of Shalim: overcast + power line west; N of Shalim: very wide unpaved light road, oil drills
SE of Ubar: NW angle + power line left + farms (vs 37: desolate/sunset); Road 43: unpaved, sunset, dust trail
NW of Ubar (unlabelled): small white markings between dashes
W of ميتان: uniquely light pavement + dunes north
Roads 45 / west-39: hilly, sunny, E-W, light shoulders + faded middle line — sun overhead = 39, sun west = 45
